channel.title: Maine Judicial Branch News & Announcements
channel.description: General Judicial Branch newsroom — a date-prefixed list of announcement links (new courthouses, eCourts rollout, bar-exam and administrative news), broader than the eFiling-specific jb-efiling-notices and the closure-focused jb-court-alerts. Same 'ul.newslist li' structure as jb-efiling-notices.

item.title: 1/29/2025: SJC invites comments on proposed amendments to the Maine Bar Admission Rules regarding application fees. Comments due by February 19, 2025.
2/13/2025: SJC invites comments on proposed amendments to the Maine Bar Admission Rules regarding admission by motion. Comments due by March 6, 2025.
2/21/2025: Chief Justice Valerie Stanfill to Deliver State of the Judiciary Address
2/26/2025: Chief Justice Valerie Stanfill's 2025 State of the Judiciary Speech
3/3/2025: Judicial Branch Warns about Court-related Scam Calls

3/11/2025: SJC invites comments on proposed amendments to the Maine Rules of Unified Criminal Procedure regarding prosecutors' oaths on complaints and warrant requests outside the warrant issuer's presence. Comments due by March 25, 2025
3/20/2025: Construction to Begin at Cumberland County Courthouse
4/5/2025: Agreement Finalized to Relocate New Hancock Judicial Center
4/14/2025: SJC invites comments on proposed Maine Rules of Family Procedure to govern family-related cases in the District Court in place of the Rules of Civil Procedure. Comments due by June 9, 2025
4/17/2025: SJC invites comments on proposed amendments to the Maine Rules of Electronic Court Systems regarding electronic signatures and oaths. Comments due by May 1, 2025.
4/30/2025: Courthouse Greeter Pilot Program Launching at the York Judicial Center in Biddeford

5/27/2025: SJC invites comments on revised proposed amendments to the order and rules governing the Committee on Judicial Conduct, including as to judicial consideration of reports of the Committee. Comments due by June 9, 2025
6/3/2025: SJC invites comments on proposed amendments to the Maine Bar Rules regarding the Board Clerk and the scope of "attorney regulation." Comments due by June 17, 2025
6/6/2025: SJC invites comments on proposed amendments to Rule 6 of the Maine Rules of Electronic Court Systems, governing access to juvenile court records. Comments due by June 12, 2025.
6/9/2025: Maine Judicial Branch Warns of Active Text Message Scam
6/30/2025: Criminal and Juvenile Case Types Launch as Part of Maine eCourts in Lewiston and Auburn Courts
7/11/2025: Volunteer Greeters Making a Positive Difference for Courthouse Visitors
7/16/2025: Judicial Branch Recognizes Employees for Outstanding Work
7/24/2025: Summer Administrative Week Scheduled July 28-August 1, 2025
8/22/2025: Madawaska District Court Closing on November 1, 2025
8/26/2025: Tips for Attorneys Brochure Now Available
9/11/2025: District Court Issues Standing Order on Payments to Court-Appointed Counsel
9/23/2025: Jury Scam Calls-Update
10/2/2025: Supreme Judicial Court to Hold Oral Arguments at Three Maine High Schools
10/8/2025: Criminal and Juvenile Case Types Launch as Part of Maine eCourts in Rumford, Farmington, and South Paris Courts
10/20/2025: "Access to Justice: From Classrooms to Courtrooms" to Be Held at the Penobscot Judicial Center on October 22, 2025
10/31/2025: Judicial Branch Applauds Attorneys Participating in the "Katahdin Counsel Recognition Program" for 2025
11/5/2025: Maine eCourts Coming to Augusta, Waterville, and Skowhegan Courts in February 2026
11/17/2025: Reminder: Virtual Presentation on Maine eCourts in Region 4 Courts (Augusta, Waterville, and Skowhegan) Tomorrow
12/1/2025: SJC invites comments on proposed amendments to the Maine Rules of Electronic Court Systems. Comments due by January 5, 2026
12/30/2025: End of the Year Holiday Schedule for the Judicial Branch
1/12/2026: Maine eCourts On Track to Launch in York County Courts this March
1/15/2026: Updates and Reminders in Preparation for Maine eCourts and eFiling in Augusta, Waterville, and Skowhegan Courts
1/20/2026: Reminder: Maine eCourts in Region 1 Virtual Presentation Today at Noon
1/28/2026: SJC adopts amendments to the Maine Rules of Electronic Court Systems
2/9/2026: Chief Justice Valerie Stanfill to Deliver State of the Judiciary Address
2/12/2026: Chief Justice Valerie Stanfill's 2026 State of the Judiciary Speech
3/5/2026: Judicial Branch Announces Plan to Complete the Rollout of Maine eCourts and eFiling by February 2027
3/12/2026: SJC invites comments on proposed amendments to the Rules of Civil Procedure on minors as parties in protection-from-abuse matters. Comments due by April 2, 2026
3/17/2026: Reminder: Maine eCourts in Region 8 (Aroostook County) Virtual Presentation March 18 at Noon
3/23/2026: Updates and Reminders in Preparation for Maine eCourts and eFiling in York County Courts
4/6/2026: Maine Judicial Branch Announces Launch of Aroostook County Adult Drug Treatment & Recovery Court
4/7/2026: SJC Comments Regarding Proposed Rule: Review of State Bar Complaints and Allegations Against Department of Justice Attorneys
4/16/2026: Judicial Branch Warns of Scam Text Notice from the "District Court of Cumberland County"
4/30/2026: Maine Judicial Branch Chief Justice Valerie Stanfill Commemorates "Law Day" 2026
5/5/2026: Maine Judicial Branch Warns of Scam Texts Sending Fake "Final Enforcement Notices" for Traffic-related Offenses
5/6/2026: Maine Judicial Branch and Maine Board of Bar Examiners Announce That NextGen Transferred Scores Will Be Accepted in 2026
5/18/2026: Maine eCourts Scheduled to be Implemented in Hancock and Washington County Courts in July
6/12/2026: Maine Judicial Branch Celebrates Employees for Outstanding Performance and Steadfast Commitment to Public Service
6/23/2026: Maine Judicial Branch Scheduled to Break Ground on New Hancock Judicial Center in July
